brand.name: Tarasi Shuttle and Transfer Services CC
brand.mission: Premium Namibia travel operating system for transfers, dispatch, tourism support and urgent client care.

reply_rules: Ask only the next needed question.
Explain price as an estimate, not a final price.
For emergency or complaint, apologize and create a ticket immediately.

hotels.name: Hilton Windhoek
hotels.pickup_advice: Pickup is usually smooth through the main entrance, but event traffic can slow access.
hotels.bot_reply: Hilton Windhoek is a strong option for business and executive guests. Tarasi can coordinate clean hotel-to-airport or city transfers.

hotels.name: Avani Windhoek Hotel & Casino
hotels.pickup_advice: CBD traffic and casino access can affect timing, so confirmed pickup time helps.
hotels.bot_reply: Avani is one of Windhoek's central hotel pickups. Tarasi can arrange direct airport or city transport with confirmed timing.

hotels.name: The Weinberg Windhoek
hotels.pickup_advice: The Weinberg area is popular for executive and VIP pickups, with smooth access for premium sedan and SUV transfers.
hotels.bot_reply: The Weinberg is a premium executive pickup point in Klein Windhoek. Tarasi usually recommends premium sedan or SUV service there.

hotels.name: Strand Hotel Swakopmund
hotels.pickup_advice: Hotel access is straightforward, but coastal fog can affect airport transfer timing.
hotels.bot_reply: Strand Hotel is one of the premium coastal pickups in Swakopmund. Tarasi can coordinate beach hotel, airport and activity transfers from there.

restaurants.name: Joe’s Beerhouse
restaurants.best_for: Namibian game meat, local atmosphere and first-time visitors
restaurants.transport_advice: Best with hotel pickup and return transfer, especially in the evening.
restaurants.bot_reply: Joe's Beerhouse is one of Windhoek's famous tourist restaurants, known for local character and game meat.

restaurants.name: The Tug Restaurant
restaurants.best_for: sunset meals, seafood and coastal dining
restaurants.transport_advice: A good option for hotel transfer in Swakopmund or Walvis Bay, especially for dinner return trips.
restaurants.bot_reply: The Tug is one of the coastal seafood favourites, popular for sunset views and a premium Swakopmund dining stop.

restaurants.name: Business lunch spots
restaurants.transport_advice: CBD and Klein Windhoek pickups work best with confirmed entry point and meeting time.
restaurants.bot_reply: For business lunch movement, Tarasi can coordinate executive hotel and office pickups in CBD or Klein Windhoek.

restaurants.name: Seafood spots
restaurants.transport_advice: Coastal return transport is helpful at night because fog can affect visibility.
restaurants.bot_reply: Swakopmund and Walvis Bay seafood spots work well with direct hotel transfer, especially for visitors unfamiliar with the coast.

restaurants.name: Nightlife spots
restaurants.best_for: evening plans, social groups and tourists
restaurants.transport_advice: Late-night trips should use a confirmed driver and shared trip details.
restaurants.bot_reply: Tarasi can help with nightlife transport in Windhoek, with confirmed pickup and safer return planning.

restaurants.name: Tourist dining spots
restaurants.best_for: first-time tourists and local food experiences
restaurants.transport_advice: Tourist dining transfers work best with hotel pickup and return coordination.
restaurants.bot_reply: Tarasi can help visitors reach popular tourist dining spots across Windhoek and the coast.

tourism_destinations.name: Sossusvlei
tourism_destinations.history: Sossusvlei sits in the Namib Desert, one of the world's oldest deserts, and is shaped by ancient dune systems and dry clay pans.
tourism_destinations.culture: It reflects Namibia's desert identity, remote travel culture and lodge-based safari rhythm.
tourism_destinations.why_people_like_it: red dunes, sunrise photography, Deadvlei and dramatic desert silence
tourism_destinations.road_condition: long-distance with tar and gravel sections
tourism_destinations.weather_warning: Midday heat is intense, and early departures are best for comfort and photography.

tourism_destinations.name: Etosha
tourism_destinations.history: Etosha National Park developed around the Etosha Pan and became one of Southern Africa's major wildlife destinations.
tourism_destinations.culture: It combines conservation travel, safari lodge culture and seasonal wildlife migration viewing.
tourism_destinations.why_people_like_it: elephants, lions, waterholes and dry-season wildlife viewing
tourism_destinations.road_condition: long highway drive with park gravel roads inside
tourism_destinations.weather_warning: Dry season is best for wildlife, but water and fuel planning matter on long legs.

tourism_destinations.name: Swakopmund
tourism_destinations.history: Swakopmund grew from a colonial port town into Namibia's best-known coastal tourism base.
tourism_destinations.culture: It blends beach life, adventure tourism, German architecture and weekend holiday travel.
tourism_destinations.why_people_like_it: cool coast weather, activities, seafood and holiday atmosphere
tourism_destinations.best_months: All year, especially September to April
tourism_destinations.weather_warning: Fog can slow coastal and airport travel, especially in the mornings.

tourism_destinations.name: Walvis Bay
tourism_destinations.history: Walvis Bay is Namibia's major harbour gateway and a key base for lagoon and marine tourism.
tourism_destinations.culture: It is known for harbour life, coastal business movement and marine activities.
tourism_destinations.why_people_like_it: lagoon views, flamingos, Sandwich Harbour and dolphin cruises
tourism_destinations.road_condition: good tar roads with coastal weather considerations
tourism_destinations.weather_warning: Fog and wind can delay departures or activity timing.

tourism_destinations.name: Windhoek
tourism_destinations.history: Windhoek is Namibia's capital city and the main gateway for business, tourism and airport movement.
tourism_destinations.culture: It mixes business travel, local city life, embassies, malls and hotel transport.
tourism_destinations.why_people_like_it: easy access to hotels, restaurants, shopping and onward travel
tourism_destinations.road_condition: city roads with traffic variations by time of day
tourism_destinations.weather_warning: Peak traffic can affect exact pickup timing in CBD and school zones.

tourism_destinations.name: Sandwich Harbour
tourism_destinations.history: Sandwich Harbour is part of Namibia's iconic dune-meets-ocean landscape south of Walvis Bay.
tourism_destinations.culture: It is tied to adventure tourism, guided 4x4 excursions and coastal dune driving.
tourism_destinations.why_people_like_it: dramatic dunes meeting the Atlantic and premium guided adventure feel
tourism_destinations.weather_warning: Tides, fog and wind matter, so departure times should stay flexible.

tourism_destinations.name: Damaraland
tourism_destinations.history: Damaraland is one of Namibia's oldest cultural and geological landscapes, known for desert-adapted wildlife and rock art.
tourism_destinations.culture: It reflects heritage travel, remote lodge touring and rugged self-drive or guided safari movement.
tourism_destinations.why_people_like_it: open landscapes, desert elephants and heritage rock art
tourism_destinations.road_condition: mixed tar and gravel with long remote legs
tourism_destinations.weather_warning: Fuel, water and early departure planning are important on this route.

tourism_destinations.name: Lüderitz
tourism_destinations.history: Lüderitz is a historic Atlantic town with colonial-era architecture and strong ties to Namibia's southern coast history.
tourism_destinations.culture: It combines coastal heritage, remote road travel and access to Kolmanskop.
tourism_destinations.why_people_like_it: historic architecture, Atlantic views and ghost-town photography
tourism_destinations.road_condition: long tar route with strong crosswinds in places
tourism_destinations.weather_warning: Southern winds can be strong, so travel timing should stay flexible.
